sense.definition: a brush used by a painter to apply paint to a surface

examples.x: The artist dipped the paintbrush into the blue paint and began to create a sky on the canvas.
She used a fine paintbrush to add delicate details to her watercolor painting.
He grabbed a wide paintbrush to quickly cover the wall with a fresh coat of paint.
The paintbrushes of different sizes and shapes were laid out on the table, ready for use.
After finishing the mural, she carefully cleaned her paintbrush to keep it in good condition.

sense.definition: a hand tool used to apply paint to large surfaces quickly and evenly
a power tool used for applying paint or other coatings to surfaces by spraying a fine mist of paint particles

examples.x: He used a paint sprayer to quickly cover the fence with a fresh coat of paint.
She cleaned the paint sprayer thoroughly after finishing the project to ensure it worked properly next time.
The paint sprayer made the job of painting the walls much faster than using a brush.
After assembling the furniture, they used a paint sprayer to add a glossy finish to the wood.

sense.definition: a flat, flexible hand tool with a blunt blade, typically made of metal or plastic, used for applying and smoothing putty, filler, or other similar materials onto surfaces

examples.x: He used a putty knife to fill the cracks in the wall before painting.
She carefully smoothed the joint compound with a putty knife to make the surface even.
The putty knife was perfect for scraping off the old paint from the window frame.
After applying the spackle, he wiped away the excess with a putty knife.
She held the putty knife at an angle to spread the filler evenly over the surface.

sense.definition: a handheld tool with a flat, rectangular blade featuring notches or teeth along one or both edges, used for applying adhesive, mortar, or other materials with consistent ridges or grooves onto surfaces

examples.x: She carefully worked the notched trowel across the wall to ensure the plaster was evenly applied.
To achieve the right coverage, he chose a notched trowel with medium-sized grooves for the thinset.
For a smooth and level surface, always use the correct size notched trowel when laying tile.
After mixing the grout, the worker used a notched trowel to apply it in even layers between the tiles.
He reached for a notched trowel to help spread the adhesive more evenly across the countertop.

sense.definition: a handheld tool used to spread, level, and smooth materials like plaster, concrete, or grout during construction or finishing work
a specialized tool with a thin, flexible blade used for applying and smoothing putty or glazing compound onto window frames or glass when installing or repairing windows

examples.x: To ensure a clean finish, she carefully ran the glazing knife along the edge of the glass.
He picked up the glazing knife to remove any excess putty from the window sill.
After applying the putty, she used the glazing knife to create a smooth, professional look.
He carefully used the glazing knife to trim the excess sealant around the edges of the new glass pane.
The carpenter used a glazing knife to smooth the putty around the window frame.

sense.definition: a handheld tool specifically designed for dispensing caulk or sealant from a cartridge

examples.x: She used a caulk gun to seal the edges of the bathtub to prevent water leaks.
The contractor handed me a caulk gun to fill the gaps between the tiles.
Before painting the window frames, he used a caulk gun to fill in the cracks.
I bought a new caulk gun because my old one was leaking sealant.
He used a caulk gun to ensure a smooth and even layer of caulk along the seams.

sense.definition: a handheld tool that melts and dispenses adhesive glue sticks for bonding materials together

examples.x: The craft project was much easier to complete with a glue gun, as the melted glue stuck instantly to the paper.
She used the glue gun to attach the decorations to the frame for her photo collage.
He carefully applied hot glue from the glue gun to the wooden pieces before pressing them together.
The kids used the glue gun to create their own holiday decorations for the classroom.
She burned her finger while working with the glue gun, so she was more careful the next time.

sense.definition: a tool specifically designed for applying adhesive onto surfaces

examples.x: She used an adhesive roller to quickly cover the entire poster with glue.
I prefer using an adhesive roller for scrapbooking because it applies glue evenly.
He grabbed the adhesive roller to apply the adhesive to the cardboard for his craft project.
With the adhesive roller, you can avoid the mess that comes with using liquid glue.

sense.definition: a tool with bristles or fibers used for applying paint, stain, or sealant to masonry surfaces, such as bricks, blocks, or concrete

examples.x: He used a masonry brush to clean the brickwork after finishing the construction.
She applied the sealant to the concrete surface with a masonry brush to ensure an even coat.
The workers used a masonry brush to prepare the brick for painting.
The contractor advised using a masonry brush to clean the newly laid mortar before it dried.
For the best results, use a masonry brush when applying water to the brick before sealing.

sense.definition: a woodworking tool that applies pressure to hold a workpiece securely during cutting or shaping operations

examples.x: The carpenter used a featherboard to keep the wood steady while cutting it on the table saw.
When working with the router, the featherboard helped ensure the piece of wood stayed in place.
I always use a featherboard when using power tools to improve control and safety.
With the featherboard in place, the saw cut through the material smoothly and accurately.

sense.definition: a handheld tool that emits hot air and is used for tasks like paint stripping, shrink-wrapping, and loosening adhesive

examples.x: The worker used a heat gun to remove the old paint from the window frame.
He carefully used the heat gun to soften the adhesive before peeling it off the surface.
The plumber grabbed a heat gun to thaw the frozen pipes in the basement.
When working on a DIY project, a heat gun can make bending plastic much easier.
She used a heat gun to remove the labels from the jars for her craft project.

sense.definition: a small portable device that produces a hot flame for heating and bonding materials together, typically used in crafts or repairs

examples.x: The technician used a soldering torch to repair the broken metal pipe.
He carefully applied the soldering torch to the joints to ensure the wires were properly connected.
The plumber used a soldering torch to fix the leak in the copper pipe under the sink.
She carefully heated the metal with the soldering torch before adding the solder to create a strong bond.

sense.definition: a hand tool with a flat, pointed, or rounded blade used for applying and spreading mortar, plaster, or other similar materials onto surfaces such as walls, floors, or ceilings during masonry or tile work
a handheld tool with a flat, pointed blade and a handle, specifically designed for spreading and shaping mortar or cement when laying bricks or blocks in masonry construction

examples.x: The mason used a brick trowel to spread mortar between the bricks for the wall.
He carefully dipped the brick trowel into the mortar before laying the next brick.
She grabbed the brick trowel to smooth out the surface of the mortar.
He cleaned his brick trowel after each layer of bricks to keep it in good condition.

sense.definition: a small, rectangular handheld tool with a flat, pointed blade and a handle, used for various tasks in masonry and tile work

examples.x: The mason used the margin trowel to smooth out the mortar in the corners of the wall.
When working on detailed tile installation, a margin trowel helps to apply grout precisely.
He grabbed the margin trowel to fill in the small gaps between the bricks.
After mixing the mortar, she used a margin trowel to scoop it onto the surface for application.

sense.definition: a specialized hand tool with a triangular-shaped blade used for applying joint compound to inside corners during drywall installation and finishing

examples.x: The painter reached for the corner trowel to touch up the edges of the drywall after the first coat dried.
To avoid a messy finish, the builder recommended using a corner trowel for precise edge work.
After applying the joint compound, she used the corner trowel to remove any excess from the corners.
The drywall contractor used a corner trowel to smooth the compound along the edges of the room.
He carefully ran the corner trowel along the corners to create sharp lines in the freshly plastered walls.

sense.definition: a small, flat-bladed handheld tool with a pointed tip and a handle, primarily used in masonry and plastering work

examples.x: The mason used the gauging trowel to mix the mortar in small batches.
He carefully measured the ingredients with the gauging trowel to ensure the right consistency.
After mixing the plaster, she used the gauging trowel to apply it to the wall.
For detailed work on the corners, the gauging trowel was the perfect tool.
